Wankhede Stadium.historical_notes: High-scoring venue with good bounce. Dew is a major factor in day-night matches, significantly aiding the side batting second.

MA Chidambaram Stadium.historical_notes: Spin-friendly surface that slows down as the match progresses. Low-scoring venue compared to others. MS Dhoni's CSK have historically dominated here.

M Chinnaswamy Stadium.historical_notes: Smallest ground in IPL with very short boundaries. Extremely high-scoring venue. Both innings tend to be high-scoring.

Eden Gardens.historical_notes: Historically a spin-friendly venue in IPL. Large crowd gives KKR a huge home advantage. Dew is a factor in evening matches.

Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium.historical_notes: Typically a good batting surface with equal assistance for pace and spin. SRH boasts a strong home record here.

Arun Jaitley Stadium.historical_notes: Usually a balanced pitch but can assist spinners. Slow outfield compared to other venues. DC have improved their home form in recent seasons.

Sawai Mansingh Stadium.historical_notes: Dry pitch that aids spinners but also good for batting. Jaipur crowd gives RR excellent home support.

Punjab Cricket Association Stadium.historical_notes: Good batting surface with some assistance for pace bowlers. PBKS are yet to win an IPL title but have often performed well at home.

Narendra Modi Stadium.historical_notes: World's largest cricket stadium. Pitches here can be tricky with uneven bounce. GT have been dominant at home since their inception.

BRSABV Ekana Cricket Stadium.historical_notes: Modern stadium with a pitch that assists spinners, especially in the second half of the tournament. LSG have a decent home record.

Himachal Pradesh Cricket Association Sta.historical_notes: Picturesque mountain venue with thin air aiding long hits. Batting-friendly surface. Known for high-scoring matches.
